methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Southern Launch is a Growth-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Lloyd Damp. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space, for a total of 13 sector participants.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-Median analysis proved inapplicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in any narrow cohort, making a median-based estimate unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring was also inapplicable because Southern Launch's revenue has not been disclosed; the public sector trades at a median P/S multiple of 25.8x across the 6 traded peers, but without the target's revenue figure, this multiple cannot be deployed.

Neither quantitative method produced a supported valuation range. The analysis reveals a structural constraint common to early-to-growth-stage space companies: insufficient disclosure density and stage-specific clustering within the peer set prevent reliable statistical triangulation. Prospective valuation work on Southern Launch would require either (1) revenue or profitability disclosure from the target, enabling public-comp multiples to be applied, or (2) identification of 3+ private comparables with disclosed valuations at similar stage and scale. Current data supports no specific valuation conclusion.
